name: vectra-vscode
description: Local-first code RAG dispatch to Claude Code

vectra.binary.markdownDescription: Path to the `vectra` binary. Defaults to `vectra` on PATH. Set this to an absolute path (e.g. `${userHome}/.local/bin/vectra`) if VS Code's environment does not see your shell PATH.

vectra.claudeModel.markdownDescription: Override the Claude model `vectra ask` invokes. Empty = use whatever is in `.vectra/config.toml` or claude's default.

vectra.effort.markdownDescription: Thinking budget passed as `--effort`. Empty = use config / claude default.

vectra.topK.markdownDescription: Number of context chunks to surface per ask. 0 = use config / built-in default of 8.

enumDescriptions: Ask before each edit (claude prompts; safest for unfamiliar code).
Auto-accept file edits in the working tree; Bash / MCP still require approval.
Explore-only — claude reads, plans and explains but does not modify any file.
Bypass every approval, including Bash and MCP. Use only in disposable environments.
vectra.permissionMode.markdownDescription: Permission mode forwarded to `claude -p` as `--permission-mode`. Mirrors Claude Code's mode picker. Default `acceptEdits` matches the wrapper's safe default.

vectra.autoIndex.markdownDescription: Reindex the workspace automatically when files change. The CLI uses BLAKE3 file hashes and skips unchanged files, so re-runs are cheap. Disable if you prefer to run `Vectra: Index workspace` manually.

vectra.autoIndexDebounceMs.markdownDescription: Quiet period in milliseconds after a file change before auto-indexing fires. Bursts (multi-file saves, git checkouts) collapse into a single index run.

enumDescriptions: Symbol-only index — no embeddings, falls back to FTS5 + tree-sitter symbols. Smallest DB, no GPU / model download required.
Qwen3 0.6B local GGUF (1024-dim). Fast on CPU, good baseline.
Qwen3 4B local GGUF (2560-dim). Higher recall, needs ~8 GB VRAM or modern CPU.
Qwen3 8B local GGUF (4096-dim). Highest recall, needs ~16 GB VRAM.
vectra.indexModel.markdownDescription: Embedding model passed to `vectra index` and `vectra ask`. Changing this requires a re-index against the new model; use the **Vectra: Re-index with model…** command to switch cleanly. Empty = symbol-only index.

enumDescriptions: No reranker. Hybrid retrieval (BM25 + embeddings + RRF) only.
Qwen3-Rerank 0.6B cross-encoder. Re-orders the top-k hits for better relevance at the cost of one extra model load.
vectra.reranker.markdownDescription: Cross-encoder reranker passed to `vectra ask`. Optional; reranking applies only to retrieval, the index itself is unaffected — flipping this on/off does not require a re-index.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run check-version && npm run sync-license && npm run compile
scripts.compile: npm run build:host && npm run build:webview
